name: anthropic-docs-v2
description: 15 Q/A pairs over the canonical Anthropic developer docs. URLs follow the .md convention published at /llms.txt (May 2026). Each item has the question, a one-sentence ground truth, and the canonical URL(s) the agent should cite. URLs are used for retrieval precision/recall. Ground truth is used by the LLM-as-judge for factuality + completeness.
notes: Anthropic recently moved their canonical docs from docs.anthropic.com (now JS-rendered) to platform.claude.com, with .md endpoints exposed at /docs/en/.../<page>.md. This dataset reflects that.

items.question: What context window does Claude Sonnet support and how is it documented?
items.ground_truth: Claude Sonnet supports a 200K (200,000) token context window; context window sizing and behavior is documented on the Context windows page.

items.question: How do I stream a response from the Messages API in Python?
items.ground_truth: Set stream=true on the Messages API call (or use client.messages.stream() as a context manager in the Python SDK) and iterate the server-sent event stream of content_block_delta events to assemble the response.

items.question: What is the difference between tool_use and tool_result content blocks?
items.ground_truth: tool_use is emitted by the assistant when it wants to invoke a tool (carries name + input + tool_use_id); tool_result is sent back by the user/client in the following turn (carries tool_use_id + content) so the model can continue.

items.question: How does prompt caching work and what content can be cached?
items.ground_truth: Mark content blocks with cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'} to cache prefixes; cached prefixes are reused across subsequent requests with significant cost and latency savings on cache hits.

items.question: What is a Claude Managed Agent and how is it different from a single Messages API call?
items.ground_truth: Managed Agents are server-side autonomous agents managed by Anthropic that handle session state, tool execution, and multi-turn loops on your behalf, contrasted with stateless Messages API calls that require the client to drive each turn.

items.question: What tool_choice options does the Messages API support?
items.ground_truth: auto (default, model decides), any (force the model to use one of the provided tools), tool (force use of a specific named tool), and none (disable tool use).

items.question: Show me a minimal Python example of calling Claude with the official SDK.
items.ground_truth: Install the anthropic package, instantiate Anthropic() (reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env), then call client.messages.create(model=..., max_tokens=..., messages=[{role:'user', content:'...'}]) and read response.content.

items.question: What does the max_tokens parameter do and what happens if it's set to 0?
items.ground_truth: max_tokens caps the response length. Setting it to 0 pre-warms the prompt cache without generating a response.

items.question: How do I enable extended thinking on a request?
items.ground_truth: Set thinking={type:'enabled', budget_tokens:N} on the Messages API call; the response will include thinking content blocks alongside the text blocks, capped at the requested token budget.

items.question: How are API rate limits structured and how can I check my current rate limit headroom?
items.ground_truth: Rate limits are per-tier and per-model; the response includes anthropic-ratelimit-* headers and you can query org/workspace rate limits via the admin endpoints.

items.question: How are PDF inputs handled by Claude?
items.ground_truth: PDFs are passed as document content blocks (base64 or URL); Claude processes both text and visual content from each page.

items.question: What is the MCP connector and what does it let me do?
items.ground_truth: The MCP connector lets the Anthropic API call out to Model Context Protocol servers (local or remote) as tools, so Claude can invoke any MCP-exposed tool inline during a conversation.

items.question: What stop_reason values can the Messages API return and what do they mean?
items.ground_truth: Common stop_reason values: end_turn (model finished), max_tokens (response truncated by the cap), stop_sequence (matched a configured stop string), tool_use (model wants to call a tool), and refusal where applicable.

items.question: Is there a way to do batch inference and what's the discount or SLA?
items.ground_truth: Yes — the Message Batches API runs up to 100,000 messages asynchronously with a 50% discount and a 24-hour SLA.

items.question: How should I think about choosing a Claude model for cost and latency?
items.ground_truth: Use Haiku for low-latency / high-volume use cases, Sonnet for balanced reasoning + cost, Opus for hardest reasoning; choice is documented alongside model overviews and the intro pages.
